title: Have I played laser tag and stuff - Request

transcript.text: Hey, what's up? I got four, like, video requests. Might as well do them, right?
So, number one from the Rat Metal Hero. I've gotten ones from him before.
So, Kyle, how are you? Next video... Oh, I'm good. Thank you.
Next video request. Did you ever play Lasertag at all?
In some sort of fun place? If so, what are your thoughts on it and why? Thanks.
Um, no, I never did. There was this place called, um, I don't even remember what it was called.
Yeah, but there was this place in Boulder that they did that, and I think, like, my brother, my little brother did it.
Um, and I would have been down to go if somebody invited me.
Um, I've never done paintball. That'd be kind of fun to do. I've seen it in the movies.
Um, uh, yeah, dude. Reenact war movies and stuff.
I'm sure I'd get killed immediately, because that stuff is probably all about skill, and aim is all about skill, too, man.
Um, I had a BB gun. I think my mom threw it away.
I was thinking about buying another BB gun and doing target practice in the house,
Because it's a fun way to just, you know, let off steam.
Um, just like, um, you know, shooting hoops.
I think I'm going to get, like, a basketball, a little hoop, you know, and throw the hoop around, you know, shoot your BB gun at the target and stuff.
We used to shoot rifle BB guns growing up, but the pistols are funner, because it's just, you know, there's more skill involved.
You can shoot it with the aim. You can shoot it from the hip.
And, um, and, uh, yeah, but I never did the laser tag.
That seems like a cool thing. I guess they're out of style.
I wonder why they still have paintballs these days, and they don't have laser tag outside.
They'd be a lot cheaper, you know, and then it could be more of a sport.
Um, yeah, but I remember when I was a kid, captured the flag, um, um, as a kid, summer camps and stuff, and that was really fun, dude.
That was really fun, because it's, like, really athletic, man, sprinting after people, and, um, and it's kind of, like, uh, has to do with real life as well, you know?

Like, like, in real life, in the history of time, you'd have, like, uh, you know, the flags, and in Roman, they had the standards, they're eagles, and they used those to, um, the guy would hold it so that the general knows where all of his regiments and divisions and stuff are, so that he can, um, and so that the people within the regiments and divisions can, and the mani, you know, the maniples and all that kind of stuff, know where to go.

The guy who's holding the flag, who's not even fighting, he's, like, the most, he's the bravest soldier, and the, and he gets the, you know, from the, he knows the signals of the horns and stuff, so then he runs in that direction, and everybody follows him, and, and so, if the bad guys get the flag, kill him and get the flag, then it's, like, a big honor.
It's kind of like the Indians cutting somebody's scalp off or something, you know, counting coup, like, injuring somebody.
But also, you can confuse the army, because now the regiment doesn't know where the hell to go, so, um, so it's kind of, the capture the flag is kind of similar to that.
Um, but, uh, but yeah, no, I never did, I never did the laser tag or anything like that.
So anyways, that's that video.
Peace out!
